doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, Dr. Lee. I started having shortness of breath and a dry cough this morning, and I also have a mild fever. It all came on pretty suddenly.

doctor_turn: Has the shortness of breath or cough changed since it started?
patient_turn: Not really, it’s pretty much the same since it started. I’m still feeling short of breath and the cough hasn’t improved.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where you feel the shortness of breath?
patient_turn: It feels like I can’t catch my breath, mostly in my chest. It’s a bit tight, but I wouldn’t say it’s painful.

doctor_turn: What does the shortness of breath feel like for you?
patient_turn: It feels like I’m struggling to take a deep breath, almost like there’s a weight on my chest. It’s uncomfortable, but not excruciating or anything.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the shortness of breath?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it started this morning, but maybe it was last night... I’m not really sure. I mean, I’ve had it before, but not like this, or maybe I have?

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the shortness of breath?
patient_turn: I first noticed it this morning. It just came on suddenly without any warning.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any seizures recently, aside from the one last night?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any seizures recently, just the one last night. It was related to my epilepsy, but I’ve been managing it otherwise.

doctor_turn: Have you missed any doses of your Keppra recently?
patient_turn: Yes, I recently missed a dose of my Keppra. I think that might have contributed to the seizure I had last night.

doctor_turn: Have you been in contact with anyone who has been ill recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t been in contact with anyone who’s been ill recently. I’ve been staying at home and working.

doctor_turn: Do you have any other medical conditions besides epilepsy?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any other medical conditions besides epilepsy. My health has been pretty good otherwise.

doctor_turn: Do you have any allergies to medications or anything else?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any allergies reported. I’ve never had any issues with medications or anything else.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have a history of epilepsy?
patient_turn: Yes, my mother has a history of epilepsy. It runs in the family a bit.

doctor_turn: Who do you live with at home?
patient_turn: I live with my wife and our 16-year-old daughter. We all get along really well!
